section_miscategorizations.text: He also reports that he previously wore compression socks.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This historical detail about prior use of compression socks is not clearly related to the current shoulder complaint and is repeated in Findings; its placement in HPI is organizationally awkward but not misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports previously wearing compression socks.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported historical behavior rather than an exam or test finding; however, it is clinically trivial and does not substantially distort the meaning of the Findings section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor will check the patient's CMP levels every couple of months.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A standing plan to monitor CMP every couple of months implies an ongoing problem (e.g., metabolic or hepatic issue) that is not mentioned in the Assessment; including this in the Plan without any corresponding problem in the Assessment can mislead the reader about the active problem list.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient should prop their feet up.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This instruction implies an underlying issue such as edema or venous insufficiency that is not reflected in the Assessment; documenting this plan without an associated assessed problem can mislead the reader about the patient's active conditions.
